rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. BANKS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 10, 2022 line 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT JAMES BANKS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as follows: demurrer to the cause of action for common counts-services rendered (sounding in contract/quasi-contract) is sustained without leave to amend. Cross-Complainant shall have leave to amend to assert a cause of action under CC 3334 alleging facts (not conclusions) as to each element of this cause of action (including facts of non-applicability of CC 3335, the Eminent Domain Law and CCP 1174). The Court notes that cross-complainant failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. In the future such failure by the cross-complainant or their counsel will result in motions being taken off calendar. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
